tossups.question: A collaborator in this architect's firm used a term for self-generating systems to title the book The Autopoiesis of Architecture. This architect's graduation project was a plan for a Suprematist hotel on Hungerford Bridge called "Malevich's Tektonik." Patrik Schumacher coined a term often used to describe this architect's Dongdaemun (dong-DAY-mun) Design Plaza, "parametricism." This architect sued the New York Review of Books after being criticized for dismissing concerns over dying construction workers building the Al-Wakrah Stadium, which will be used for the 2022 World Cup. Two rocks being smoothed by the Pearl River inspired the flowing lines in the design of this architect's Guangzhou Opera House, which contributed to their nickname of the "queen of the curve." The MAXXI Museum was designed by, for 10 points, what Iraqi-British architect?
tossups.question_sanitized: A collaborator in this architect's firm used a term for self-generating systems to title the book The Autopoiesis of Architecture. This architect's graduation project was a plan for a Suprematist hotel on Hungerford Bridge called "Malevich's Tektonik." Patrik Schumacher coined a term often used to describe this architect's Dongdaemun (dong-DAY-mun) Design Plaza, "parametricism." This architect sued the New York Review of Books after being criticized for dismissing concerns over dying construction workers building the Al-Wakrah Stadium, which will be used for the 2022 World Cup. Two rocks being smoothed by the Pearl River inspired the flowing lines in the design of this architect's Guangzhou Opera House, which contributed to their nickname of the "queen of the curve." The MAXXI Museum was designed by, for 10 points, what Iraqi-British architect?

tossups.question: This architect used the concept of a “Metonymic Landscape” for Seoul’s Dongdaemun Design Plaza. This architect’s design for the Cardiff Bay Opera House lacked financial support and has never been realised, and plans for a bicycle helmet-shaped stadium in Meiji Park by this architect have also been scrapped. (*) This architect’s completed projects include the BMW Central Building in Leipzig, and the “twin boulders” of Guangzhou Opera House. The designer of the London 2012 Aquatics Centre and the 2022 FIFA World Cup Stadium, for 10 points, name this Pritzker-winning female architect.
tossups.question_sanitized: This architect used the concept of a "Metonymic Landscape" for Seoul's Dongdaemun Design Plaza. This architect's design for the Cardiff Bay Opera House lacked financial support and has never been realised, and plans for a bicycle helmet-shaped stadium in Meiji Park by this architect have also been scrapped. (*) This architect's completed projects include the BMW Central Building in Leipzig, and the "twin boulders" of Guangzhou Opera House. The designer of the London 2012 Aquatics Centre and the 2022 FIFA World Cup Stadium, for 10 points, name this Pritzker-winning female architect.

packet.name: 09 Minarik et al. + Nguyen et al.

tossups.question: This artist created a painting called The Peak Project depicting an unrealized project intended for the hills of Kowloon. A partner in this architect’s namesake firm coined the term parametricism to describe their work. This architect designed a Z-shaped secondary school in London called the Evelyn Grace Academy. This architect designed a building in Wolfsburg which rests on huge concrete stilts, allowing visitors to walk underneath it. This architect of the (*) Phaeno Science Centre also created a building with a wave-shaped roof which had spectator wings added for the 2012 Olympics, the London Aquatics Centre. This architect used floating stairs in a modern art museum in Rome called the Maxxi. For 10 points, name this postmodernist architect, a woman born in Baghdad.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist created a painting called The Peak Project depicting an unrealized project intended for the hills of Kowloon. A partner in this architect's namesake firm coined the term parametricism to describe their work. This architect designed a Z-shaped secondary school in London called the Evelyn Grace Academy. This architect designed a building in Wolfsburg which rests on huge concrete stilts, allowing visitors to walk underneath it. This architect of the (*) Phaeno Science Centre also created a building with a wave-shaped roof which had spectator wings added for the 2012 Olympics, the London Aquatics Centre. This architect used floating stairs in a modern art museum in Rome called the Maxxi. For 10 points, name this postmodernist architect, a woman born in Baghdad.

tossups.question: This artist used a single piece of polyurethane to create the furniture piece Aqua Table, whose three curves are "undulations" representing waves. This designer has made numerous interlocking sofas and other "floating" tables and chairs as part of a collection called Z.Scape, which was largely inspired by this architect’s design for a museum in Wolfsberg, Germany that allows for vehicles and pedestrians to move underneath it. Another building by this creator of the Phaeno Science Center was inspired by the curvy shape of the dhow fishing (*) boat; the death of migrant workers during that building’s construction led to a controversy in which this architect sued the New York Review of Books for defamation. The concept of boulders being erosively smoothed by a river inspired this architect's "twin boulders" design for a mostly glass music center on the Pearl River. The 2022 FIFA World Cup stadium and the Guangzhou Opera House were designed by, for 10 points, what recently-deceased Iranian architect?
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist used a single piece of polyurethane to create the furniture piece Aqua Table, whose three curves are "undulations" representing waves. This designer has made numerous interlocking sofas and other "floating" tables and chairs as part of a collection called Z.Scape, which was largely inspired by this architect's design for a museum in Wolfsberg, Germany that allows for vehicles and pedestrians to move underneath it. Another building by this creator of the Phaeno Science Center was inspired by the curvy shape of the dhow fishing (*) boat; the death of migrant workers during that building's construction led to a controversy in which this architect sued the New York Review of Books for defamation. The concept of boulders being erosively smoothed by a river inspired this architect's "twin boulders" design for a mostly glass music center on the Pearl River. The 2022 FIFA World Cup stadium and the Guangzhou Opera House were designed by, for 10 points, what recently-deceased Iranian architect?

tossups.question: This architect designed spiral-shaped, pebbled boots for Lacoste, as well as created three organic-shaped white parasols in the Lilas installation, the first iteration of the Serpentine Gallery’s yearly pavilion tradition. Wings were removed from a British facility designed by this architect that retains a concrete ceiling that slopes downwards towards an Olympic swimming pool situated between two walls of windows; that building is the London (*) Aquatics Center. A design by this architect called for two parallel tapered points on the end of a large ovoid structure, which Arata Isozaki said looked like “a turtle waiting for Japan to sink so that it can swim away” - that building wasn’t built, though this architect’s plan for another stupidly extravagant stadium continues to cost the lives of underpaid South Asian workers. For 10 points, name this architect of the scrapped 2020 Tokyo Stadium and the vaginesque 2022 Al-Wakrah stadium in Qatar, a female Pritzker winner who died recently.
tossups.question_sanitized: This architect designed spiral-shaped, pebbled boots for Lacoste, as well as created three organic-shaped white parasols in the Lilas installation, the first iteration of the Serpentine Gallery's yearly pavilion tradition. Wings were removed from a British facility designed by this architect that retains a concrete ceiling that slopes downwards towards an Olympic swimming pool situated between two walls of windows; that building is the London (*) Aquatics Center. A design by this architect called for two parallel tapered points on the end of a large ovoid structure, which Arata Isozaki said looked like "a turtle waiting for Japan to sink so that it can swim away" - that building wasn't built, though this architect's plan for another stupidly extravagant stadium continues to cost the lives of underpaid South Asian workers. For 10 points, name this architect of the scrapped 2020 Tokyo Stadium and the vaginesque 2022 Al-Wakrah stadium in Qatar, a female Pritzker winner who died recently.

bonuses.leadin: This architect's first constructed building was for an in-house company fire station after a factory burned down. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This architect's first constructed building was for an in-house company fire station after a factory burned down. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this architect who designed the Vitra Fire station, as well as the Guangzhou Opera House.
The Vitra Fi<u>re Stati</u>on was built next to a factory that manufactured these items designed by Charles and Ray Eames. Eero Saarinen designed a tulip example of these objects.

Robert<u> Vent</u>uri designed Fire Station Number 4 for this city. Cummins President J. Irwin Miller heavily invested in public architecture in this city, including commissions for I. M. Pei and Cesar Pelli.
parts_sanitized: Name this architect who designed the Vitra Fire station, as well as the Guangzhou Opera House.
The Vitra Fire Station was built next to a factory that manufactured these items designed by Charles and Ray Eames. Eero Saarinen designed a tulip example of these objects.

Robert Venturi designed Fire Station Number 4 for this city. Cummins President J. Irwin Miller heavily invested in public architecture in this city, including commissions for I. M. Pei and Cesar Pelli.

bonuses.leadin: An architect sought to blend the designs of a tower and podium for a building in this city. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this city where the Innovation Tower was built for its Polytechnic University. A distinctive I. M. Pei skyscraper in this city received criticism for the “X” shapes formed by its exterior of adjacent white triangles.
This architect, the “queen of the curve,” designed the Innovation Tower. The London Aquatics Center for the 2012 Olympics and Rome’s MAXXI museum were also designed by this British-Iraqi architect.

Hadid used two rocks being washed away by the Pearl River as the inspiration for a building of this type in Guangzhou. Sails inspired the iconic white roof of one of these buildings in Sydney designed by Jørn Utzon.
answers: Hong Kong (The Pei building is the Bank of China Tower.)
Zaha Hadid [or Dame Zaha Mohammad Hadid DBE RA]
opera house [or the Guangzhou Opera House; or the Sydney Opera House]
answers_sanitized: Hong Kong (The Pei building is the Bank of China Tower.)
Zaha Hadid [or Dame Zaha Mohammad Hadid DBE RA]
opera house [or the Guangzhou Opera House; or the Sydney Opera House]

bonuses.leadin: Since commissioning this architect in 2000, the London organization Serpentine has commissioned a different architect to create a temporary summer pavilion every year. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this architect of the Guangzhou (“gwang-joh”) Opera House and the London Aquatics Centre, the latter of which was built for the 2012 Summer Olympics.
The 2017 pavilion was designed by Diébédo Francis Kéré (“d’yuh-BED-oh francis KAY-ray”), an architect born in this African country, who became the first African winner of the Pritzker Prize in 2022.

The 2003 Serpentine Pavilion was designed by Oscar Niemeyer (“nee-uh-MAH-yer”), who is best known for working with Lucio (“loo-SEE-oh”) Costa and designing many buildings in this South American planned capital city.

bonuses.leadin: Constructivist art inspired the sharply angular overhang of this museum’s concrete facade. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this parametricist museum built on top of a former military barracks. This museum’s stark white interior contrasts with its weaving and underlit black staircases, which lie below a ceiling of flowing concrete beams and louvered skylights.
This architect’s subterranean Chichu Art Museum lets in light through concrete-reinforced geometric openings, a technique he also used in his Church of the Light.

Lina Bo Bardi designed an art museum in this country without interior walls that displays painting on crystal easels throughout its open plan. Oscar Niemeyer designed many buildings in this country's planned capital.
answers: MAXXI [accept Museo nazionale delle arti del XXI secolo; accept Italy’s National Museum of 21st Century Arts; prompt on National Museum of 21st Century Arts] (by Zaha Hadid)
answers_sanitized: MAXXI [accept Museo nazionale delle arti del XXI secolo; accept Italy's National Museum of 21st Century Arts; prompt on National Museum of 21st Century Arts] (by Zaha Hadid)

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about the links between architecture and shoe design, for 10 points each.
parts: Le Corbusier inspired the Bata shoe company’s functionalist redesign of a city in this country. “Fred and Ginger” was the original name of Frank Gehry’s Dancing House in this country’s capital, Prague.

In 2012, this architect teamed up with Converse to release Chuck Taylor trainers printed with a poem about his longing for the “free and sensual curve.” This architect designed the headquarters of the French Communist Party while in exile in Paris.

In 2015, this architect was one of five invited by Rem Koolhaas’s (“KOHL-hahss’s”) company United Nude to design 3D-printed women’s shoes. This Deconstructivist architect, who also created shoes for Melissa, designed the Guǎngzhōu Opera House.
answers: Oscar Niemeyer [or Oscar Ribeiro de Almeida Niemeyer Soares Filho] (The two collaborated because they both celebrated their 104th birthdays in 2012.)

packet.name: 06 - William and Mary A, Florida A, UIUC B, Virginia Tech A

answers_sanitized: Czechia [or Czech Republic or Ceska republika; prompt on Czechoslovakia]

Zaha Hadid [or Dame Zaha Mohammad Hadid]

parts: Name this Canadian province that is also home to Casa Loma and the CN Tower.

Ma Yansong’s designs are often influenced by these courtyards commonly found in Beijing. The entrances to these complexes were made at their southeast corner to correspond with and symbolize the natural element of wind.

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, answer the following about honeycombs in Middle Eastern architecture:
parts: This architect’s firm designed the white honeycomb exterior of Riyadh’s huge King Abdullah Petroleum Research Center. This Iraqi-British architect designed the London Aquatics Center for the 2012 Olympics.
The interiors of these elaborately ornamented domed vaults, ubiquitous in Islamic architecture, are frequently compared to honeycombs or stalactites.

The entrance to this pavilion is surrounded by a large iwan, or open vault, whose façade is decorated with a golden-inlaid honeycomb design. This Iranian pleasure-palace is found at the end of a long pool surrounded by a char bagh garden.
answers: Chehel Sotoun (“shuh-HEL soo-TOON”) [prompt on Forty Columns] (The palace is in Isfahan.)

bonuses.leadin: It's time to go to the opera! So for 10 points each, answer the following about opera houses.
parts: This city's opera house took Ove Arup's engineering firm 14 years to complete, including experiencing difficulties building the "shells" designed by Jørn Utzon.

This architect's design for the Guangzhou Opera House was inspired by the banks of the Pearl River. This first woman to win the Pritzker Prize also designed the Broad (rhymes with "load") Art Museum at Michigan State University.
Lyon's opera house was designed by this French architect, who it is named after. This 2008 Pritzker winner designed the bullet-shaped Torre Agbar in Barcelona and the Guthrie Theater in Minneapolis.

bonuses.leadin: A building under construction in this city was previously known as the Scorpion Tower, but has been renamed the 1000 Museum. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this city, home to the Adrienne Arsht Center for the Performing Arts designed by César Pelli, and a concert hall designed by Frank Gehry called the New World Center.

The 1000 Museum in Miami was designed by this Iraqi-born architect who died in 2016. Her work is based around computer-generated parametric curves, as in her cultural center in Baku, the MAXXI museum in Venice, and the Zaragoza Bridge Pavilion.

This Swiss architecture firm designed an experimental parking garage at 1111 Lincoln Street in Miami that features irregular trapezoidal supports and glass-enclosed storefronts and apartments. They also designed the “Bird’s Nest” for the Beijing Olympics.

parts: Name this late Iraqi-born British architect whose design for the Guangzhou (gwahng-joh) Opera House resembles two pebbles on the shore of the Pearl River. In 2004, she became the first woman to receive the Pritzker Prize.

With Ben van Berkel, Hadid designed a pair of color-changing pavilions to commemorate the centennial of Daniel Burnham’s plan for this city. Fazlur Khan designed this city’s Sears Tower, now known as Willis Tower.

Hadid designed the “nerve-center” of a factory complex meant to bring together blue-collar and white-collar employees of this company. This company’s headquarters resembles four vertical cylinders arranged in a tight square.
answers: BMW [or Bavarian Motor Works; or Bayerische Motoren Werke AG]
